title: 10 possible side effects of Boswellia serrata (frankincense)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Boswellia serrata causes stomach upset due to its ability to irritate the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The boswellic acids present in Boswellia serrata can stimulate the production of acid and pepsin in the stomach, leading to heartburn, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties may cause stomach pain or cramping as it reduces inflammation in the digestive system. These symptoms are typically mild and temporary, but they can be uncomfortable for some individuals taking Boswellia serrata supplements.

Boswellia serrata induces allergic reactions in some individuals. This is due to the presence of a compound called boswellic acid, which can trigger an immune response and cause symptoms such as hives, itching, and swelling. Additionally, frankincense contains other bioactive molecules that may interact with an individual's unique genetic profile, further increasing the risk of an allergic reaction. As a result, some people may experience adverse reactions when using Boswellia serrata supplements or applying frankincense topically to the skin.

Boswellia serrata triggers headaches due to its ability to thin the blood and increase blood flow to the brain. This increased blood flow can cause a temporary dilation of blood vessels, leading to pain and discomfort in some individuals. Additionally, Boswellia serrata may also stimulate the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause blood vessel constriction, further contributing to headache symptoms.

Boswellia serrata stimulates insomnia due to its potential ability to increase levels of cortisol, a hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles. As cortisol levels rise, it can interfere with the body's natural sleep patterns, making it difficult for individuals to fall asleep or stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, Boswellia serrata may also cause increased heart rate and blood pressure, further contributing to disrupted sleep patterns and insomnia.

Boswellia serrata increases appetite in some individuals due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. By decreasing inflammation in the digestive tract, Boswellia serrata may stimulate the production of hormones that regulate appetite, leading to an increase in food cravings. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders, which can sometimes be accompanied by increased hunger.

Boswellia serrata reduces blood pressure due to its ability to relax blood vessels and reduce peripheral resistance. This occurs through the suppression of the sympathetic nervous system's activity, which helps to decrease vasoconstriction and increase vasodilation. As a result, systolic and diastolic blood pressures are lowered, potentially leading to orthostatic hypotension in some individuals.

Boswellia serrata decreases white blood count. This side effect is thought to be caused by boswellic acids, which can suppress the activity of immune cells and lead to a decrease in white blood cell production. Additionally, Boswellia serrata may also increase the production of free radicals, which can further exacerbate this effect. As a result, people taking Boswellia serrata supplements may experience an increased risk of infection due to their decreased ability to fight off pathogens.

Boswellia serrata activates dizziness in some individuals due to its ability to thin the blood and reduce inflammation. As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, boswellia can cause a drop in blood pressure, leading to feelings of lightheadedness or dizziness. Additionally, the herb's potential interaction with certain medications, such as blood thinners and sedatives, may also contribute to this side effect.

Boswellia serrata treats nausea due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. As an anti-inflammatory agent, it may cause stomach upset or nausea in some individuals, particularly when taken in high doses or for extended periods of time. This side effect is thought to be caused by the herb's interaction with stomach acid and digestive enzymes, leading to discomfort and potentially even vomiting.

Boswellia serrata enhances anxiety due to its ability to stimulate the nervous system and increase cortisol levels in the body. This can lead to feelings of restlessness, irritability, and an increased sense of alertness. Additionally, Boswellia's potent anti-inflammatory properties may exacerbate existing anxiety conditions by altering brain chemistry and disrupting normal neurotransmitter functioning, further contributing to heightened anxiety states.

intro: Boswellia serrata has some side effects when used improperly, such as nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea. These adverse reactions can occur due to the herb's high concentration of boswellic acids, which may irritate the digestive tract or interact with medications. For individuals experiencing these symptoms, it can be challenging to maintain a healthy diet, causing fatigue, mood disturbances, and decreased productivity. Furthermore, frequent stomach discomfort can lead to social withdrawal, anxiety, and depression.
intro_desc: Boswellia serrata has some side effects when used improperly, such as diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea. These side effects are often caused by excessive dosing, long-term use, or interacting with medications. For instance, a person with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) may experience worsening symptoms like persistent diarrhea or abdominal pain, disrupting their daily activities and sleep patterns, and affecting their overall quality of life.
